highlights.text: Institutions leave their footprint on the chart when they buy a dip and it comes in the form of my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “One could wait for a Failed Breakdown of Monday’s 6835 daily low.” We recovered 6835 at 9:40PM, and we were off to the races.
Unfortunately closing at the high of days yesterday, there was little to do but wait for the next flush and insitutional accumulation event (Failed Breakdown).
I stated at 4pm yesterday: “My job is to wait for ES to flush, get everyone bearish, trap below a low, then I get long with institutions on the Failed Breakdown.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “Below there, a Failed Breakdown of today’s 6830 daily low is an option.” After this trigger, we were off to the races to 6900+ today.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the variety of Failed Breakdowns we had Friday-yesterday that setup the FOMC squeeze.
Institutions often accumulate via multiple Failed Breakdowns over time and it is essential to spot them.
As I stated yesterday, next targets up were “6915, 6924/31.

post_title: Bulls Keep Buying Every Dip. Can They Finish The Job and Take SPX To All Time Highs Now? December 12 Plan
